caption_highlights.description: Structured caption analysis (axes, series, error bars...).

caption_verbatim.description: Verbatim figure caption from the source publication.

citation.description: Bibliographic metadata for literature-derived assets (authors, title, journal, year, volume, pages, doi).

attribution.description: WHO: uploader, owner, analyst, curator. uploaded_by is SERVER-STAMPED from the authenticated identity at ingestion — client-supplied values are overwritten (tamper-proof attribution). Per-output analysis attribution lives in descriptors.outputs[].generated_by; this block is record-level. Decided by D. Sokaras 2026-06-15.

uploaded_by.description: Authenticated identity that submitted this record. Set by the server; any client value is overwritten.

computation.description: METHOD DECLARATION ONLY (ADR per computation verdict, 2026-06-13): computation declares HOW — never numerical results. Scalar results are descriptors (kind=theoretical_metric); simulated spectra/curves are measurement.series with role=simulated_observable; structures/INCARs/raw totals are assets (interim: JSON-strings in system.configuration).

corrections_applied.description: Energy corrections declared applied (booleans): dispersion, entropy, ZPE, PCET, solvation, ...

energy_reference.description: Energy reference convention (e.g. 'RHE via CHE', 'gas-phase H2/H2O').

method.description: Declared theory level: method family, functional, code details.

cutoff_eV.description: Planewave kinetic energy cutoff in eV.

functional_name.description: Specific functional (e.g., PBE, RPBE, BEEF-vdW, HSE06).

pseudopotential.description: Pseudopotential type (e.g., PAW, ultrasoft, norm_conserving, GBRV, SG15).

notes.description: Free-text declaration notes (hypothesis context, calculation intent).

SHE_work_function_eV.description: Work function of SHE used for the absolute potential scale (typically 4.4-4.6 eV).

target_potential_V_SHE.description: Target electrode potential for constant-potential (GC-DFT) calculations.

reaction.description: Elementary step, e.g. CO* + CO* -> OCCO*

anolyte.description: Anolyte specification when different from catholyte. Same shape as electrolyte; open for composition extras (e.g. nitrate_concentration_ppm_N).

co_reactant.description: Co-fed reactant in the electrolyte (e.g. nitrate for urea co-electrosynthesis). Open for concentration extras.

applied_to_reported_potential.description: Whether potential_setpoint_V already includes the correction.

cell_resistance_ohm.description: Uncompensated resistance used for correction (promoted: 119 records already carried it ad hoc).

potential_setpoint_V.description: PRIMARY, immutable: the as-commanded potential on the scale declared by potential_scale, exactly as measured/reported. Never overwritten by conversion — the RHE projection lives in potential_vs_RHE.

potential_vs_RHE.description: DERIVED canonical query axis: the operating potential in V vs RHE (setpoint for potentiostatic, steady-state measured potential for galvanostatic). NEVER the primary — primaries (potential_setpoint_V, descriptors, channels) are immutable on their as-measured scale. value_V may be null ONLY with an explicit not_* rhe_basis, so records can never silently fall off the potential axis.

conversion.description: Frozen provenance. Inputs are snapshotted (pH_used, offset) so later context edits cannot silently invalidate the derivation.

formula.description: e.g. 'E_RHE = E_meas + offset_V_vs_SHE + 0.0591*pH'. Verbatim source formula when reported_by_source.

input_path.description: Path of the converted PRIMARY, e.g. 'context.electrochemistry.potential_setpoint_V' or 'descriptors:steady_state_potential'.

rhe_conversion_offset_V.description: Single additive calibrated conversion constant: E_RHE = E_measured + rhe_conversion_offset_V. Bundles the reference-electrode offset AND the Nernst pH term, calibrated against a master reference (accounts for individual electrode drift). When present, use it instead of the offset+pH route and set rhe_basis=derived_calibrated. Origin: Caltech HTE per-session calibration values (2026-06-12).

ir_corrected.description: Whether value_V includes iR correction. May differ from the primary.

rhe_basis.description: Trust tier of value_V. measured_direct: physical RHE reference. derived_calibrated: converted with a calibrated offset. reported_as_RHE: source stated RHE, conversion unrecoverable. derived_nominal: converted with tabulated/assumed offset + nominal pH. not_*: value_V is null, reason explicit. not_applicable = a half-cell RHE projection does not exist for this device (full 2-electrode electrolyzer); value_V is null.

uncertainty_V.description: Estimated uncertainty of value_V (offset ambiguity + pH basis), in V.

reaction_notes.description: Nuance the reaction enum cannot express.

reference_electrode.description: Structured specification of the physical reference electrode the PRIMARY potentials were measured against. Required when potential_scale names a physical electrode. Supersedes the free-text system.configuration.reference_electrode.

filling_solution.description: The INTERNAL solution inside the reference electrode body (e.g. '3.0 M KCl') — NOT the cell electrolyte (which lives in context.electrochemistry.electrolyte). The filling sets the reference potential via the Cl- activity at the Ag/AgCl wire: sat. KCl -> 0.197 V, 3 M -> 0.210 V, 1 M -> 0.235 V vs SHE. Vendor naming 'Ag/AgCl (3 M KCl)' refers to this filling.

offset_V_vs_SHE.description: E0 of this reference vs SHE. Ag/AgCl 3.0M KCl: 0.210; 1.0M KCl: 0.235; sat.: 0.197.

feed.description: Feed must be an object with phase and composition. Do not use a bare string.

CO_percent.description: CO fraction of the feed in percent (operando feed-variation studies).

at.description: Operating point within a series. Sample identity, operating potential, electrolyte, and cell type all live in their canonical slots — never as 'at' keys.

name.description: Descriptor name. Standard form: <class> or <class>.<product> or <class>.<product>.<context>. Rejects known anti-patterns: '_magnitude' suffix (use negative values in canonical name), '_ratio'/'_normalized' (derive downstream), 'current_fraction.*' (mechanism-attribution derivative), '*.partial_sum_*'. See Controlled-Vocabulary wiki for canonical class list.

relative_to.description: For differential descriptors, the reference state this is measured against. Either a string label ('IrO2', 'pure_Cu') or a structured object referencing another ISAAC record.

uncertainty.description: Uncertainty of the value. Prefer sigma+unit; basis documents provenance (reported, digitization, assumed). Do NOT encode "not reported" as sigma:0 — state it in basis.

sigma.description: Standard deviation. null = not reported (prefer basis: not_reported).

value.description: The asserted value. Must not be null — a descriptor without a value is not a scientific claim.

generated_utc.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the descriptors were generated. Placeholder strings are not allowed.

source_figure_refs.description: Source figures these descriptors were derived from.

processing.description: Processing chain. Canonical fields: steps[] (controlled vocabulary) + recipe_link. NOTE: block intentionally not locked yet — CatHub ingestion metadata (cathub_*) relocation pending; do not add new ad-hoc keys.

steps.description: Ordered processing steps from the measurement.processing.steps vocabulary.

assumptions.description: Documented judgment calls made during record creation.

evidence.description: REQUIRED in practice when status != valid: what is compromised/failed and why. "N/A" defeats the purpose.

string_values.description: Categorical channel values when the channel is non-numeric.

conditions.description: Operating conditions specific to this series when they differ from context (e.g. per-series control_mode in multi-step protocols).

conditions_inherited_from.description: Provenance: series/record whose conditions this series inherits (record-split bookkeeping).

record_domain.description: Scientific domain of the record content.

composition.description: Open by design: per-material composition entries (at%, loadings, layer thicknesses). Keys should be self-describing with units in the name (e.g. loading_nmol_Ni_mm2, Cu_at_fraction).

electrode_type.description: Type of electrode or substrate configuration (e.g., GDE, RDE, RRDE, foil, MEA).

geometry.description: Open by design: geometric descriptors. geometric_area_cm2 is the canonical electrode-area key.

library.description: High-throughput library membership: locates this sample on a composition-spread plate. Required context for HTE records (e.g. scanning droplet cell screening).

sample_id.description: Stable identifier of the PHYSICAL sample/electrode. Two records share a sample_id if and only if they measured the same physical object — this is the basis that gives same_sample_as links their meaning. Format free (lab convention), but must be stable across all records of that object. Decided 2026-06-12.

configuration.description: THE designated open extension namespace: instrument/station/beamline-specific configuration that does not generalize across facilities (slits, pass energies, GC columns, channel IDs, logbook fields...). Anything that DOES generalize belongs in a schema field — request one.

technique.description: Primary technique or computational method used to produce this record.

tags.description: Free-form, user-assigned grouping labels. A record may carry SEVERAL (it can belong to a broad archive AND a specific campaign). This is how a user groups an arbitrary SET of records at any granularity (campaign, material system, study) without a rigid hierarchy — finer groupings are just more specific tags. Recommended convention: lowercase, hyphenated, scoped, e.g. 'jcap-hte', 'nifecoce-oer-screen', 'caltech-2014'. The physical HTE hierarchy (plate/spot) stays in sample.library.{plate_id,sample_no}. A non-empty tags array satisfies the NO_LINKS grouping nudge.

derived_from_record_id.description: DEPRECATED: use links[] with rel=derived_from. Retained for 215 legacy records pending the links repair migration.

revision_note.description: Why the record was last revised.

split_operation.description: Provenance note when this record was produced by splitting a multi-condition source record.
